what_it_does: Workshop.jsx is the main React canvas for the ConceptV design system. It lists doc types and existing docs, creates new docs (optionally with AI drafting), and provides an editing surface for deck/brochure pages built from draggable blocks. It also delegates widget and wizard docs to global builder components and wires in AI candidate galleries, variables, themes, layouts, undo/redo, and save-as-template flows.
observations: Built-in DOC_TYPES array marks deck, brochure, widget, and wizard as active while website, social, email, and video are flagged coming-soon.
useExtraDocTypes pulls additional doc types from window.CV_REGISTRY with provenance user or vi and renders them separately.
The component relies heavily on window globals: React, WS_*, CV_REGISTRY, WidgetBuilder, WizardBuilder, claude, dsaToast, and others.
State is managed through many useState hooks plus useRef stacks for undo/redo snapshots of JSON-serialized docs.
Section-level operations include add, remove, move, duplicate, lock, refine via Claude, generate variations, and pick variations.
Drag-and-drop reordering is implemented for both sections within a slide and slides within the deck strip/outline.
A central runGallery function opens a candidate gallery and streams AI-generated options for transforms, alternates, insertions, and layout proposals.
The VariablesRail lets users add workspace variables, edit values, see live reference counts, and run a Vi audit to find candidates.
The save-as-template flow asks WS_AI.extractTemplate, previews variables and materialized output, then stores the template.
Widget and wizard docs branch to window.WidgetBuilder and window.WizardBuilder instead of using the shared section editor.
standouts: The runGallery abstraction unifies many AI interactions into one candidate-pick UI pattern.
Undo/redo is implemented by capturing full JSON snapshots of the doc on every change.
Variables, suggestions, and template extraction are embedded directly inside the canvas rather than in separate modules.
The file uses global window APIs as its primary plugin/dependency-injection mechanism.
conventions: React hooks are aliased with a _w suffix (useState_w, useEffect_w, etc.).
Subcomponents are defined as plain functions below the main component; no TypeScript or prop-types are used.
Functional updates are passed through updateDoc, which applies an updater and calls saveDoc.
IDs are generated with prefixes plus Date.now().
Inline AI prompts repeat brand-voice rules and block-shape instructions.
concerns: The file is large and mixes list view, editor, AI flows, variables, templates, and export controls in one module.
Heavy reliance on window globals makes behavior hard to trace, type-check, or unit test in isolation.
Undo/redo JSON snapshots may be memory-heavy and cannot preserve non-serializable UI state.
Inline AI prompts are duplicated and not centralized; parseJsonLoose is a brittle recovery strategy.
Widget/wizard branches assume window.WidgetBuilder and window.WizardBuilder exist without visible fallback.
notes: This is the kitchen-sink editing surface that glues the ConceptV block system, AI assistant, workspace variables, and template machinery into a single user-facing workshop canvas.
questions: Where are window.WS_BLOCKS, WS_LAYOUTS, WS_THEMES, WS_MOTIFS, and related registries defined and what contracts do they enforce?
How does saveDoc persist docs and how are they loaded back into the Workshop?
What is the exact contract of window.WS_AI.generateCandidatesStream, invertDiff, and applyDiff?
How do registry doc types map to editor/renderer behavior given the family fallback logic?
What happens if window.WidgetBuilder or window.WizardBuilder are missing at runtime?
Are undo/redo stacks scoped per doc or global, and how do they behave across doc switches?
purpose_vs_actual: The header comment describes it as the visual canvas for composing real deliverables. The file fulfills that but also absorbs AI drafting, variables, templates, and export controls, making it broader than a pure canvas.
apparent_themes: AI-assisted composition as a first-class editing primitive.
Block-based document model with direct manipulation.
Workspace-wide variables for consistency across docs.
intention_signals: The file is trying to be the single unified place where a user moves from an idea to a finished deliverable by choosing a doc type, drafting with AI, arranging blocks, applying themes, and saving reusable templates.
novelty: The central runGallery abstraction that turns nearly every AI-driven choice—block alternates, layout proposals, whole-doc transforms, field rewrites—into the same candidate-pick UI pattern.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, user-facing orchestration layer that connects doc types, block editors, AI generation, variables, themes, and templates into one interactive canvas, demonstrating a pattern of treating AI suggestions as a reusable candidate gallery.
summary_for_embedding: Workshop.jsx is a React canvas component for the ConceptV design system that lists, creates, and edits deck/brochure documents via draggable blocks, delegates widget/wizard editing to global builders, and integrates AI drafting, candidate galleries, variables, themes, layouts, undo/redo, and template extraction.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to provide a unified, AI-assisted workshop surface where users compose real deliverables from typed blocks and save them as reusable templates.

symbols.does: Subscribes to the global CV_REGISTRY and returns user/vi-authored doc types at layer=doc.
symbols.description: This hook listens for registry updates via window.CV_REGISTRY.subscribe, queries layer='doc' entries, and filters to provenance 'user' or 'vi' so custom types can appear in the doc picker alongside built-ins.

symbols.does: Main canvas component that orchestrates listing, creating, editing, and AI-assisting ConceptV docs.
symbols.description: Workshop switches between a list view for doc selection and an edit view for deck/brochure pages, while delegating widget/wizard docs to global builders. It manages state for drafts, sections, pages, themes, variables, candidate galleries, templates, and undo/redo.

symbols.does: Closes the whole-doc transform menu when a click reaches the document body.
symbols.description: A small event handler attached inside a useEffect whenever transformMenuOpen is true; it dismisses the menu on any document click.

symbols.does: Starts the save-as-template flow by asking Vi to extract variables from the current doc.
symbols.description: It sets a loading preview state, calls window.WS_AI.extractTemplate, and opens a modal. If extraction fails it falls back to saving a plain copy of the doc as a template.

symbols.does: Persists the prepared template preview through the saveTemplate callback.
symbols.description: When the user confirms the modal, this function builds a template object from templatePreview and passes it to saveTemplate, then clears the modal and shows a toast.

symbols.does: Deep-clones an object using JSON serialization.
symbols.description: A small utility used to copy doc data before saving as a template; it does not preserve functions, dates, or other non-JSON values.

symbols.does: Opens the central candidate gallery and streams AI-generated candidates for a target.
symbols.description: It creates a gallery state, calls window.WS_AI.generateCandidatesStream with callbacks that append candidates and clear the busy flag, and handles errors by toasting a failure message.

symbols.does: Applies the candidate selected from the gallery.
symbols.description: If an override handler exists it calls it; otherwise it inverts the candidate diff and applies it to the doc through saveDoc, then closes the gallery.

symbols.does: Regenerates or extends candidates inside the open gallery based on a user hint.
symbols.description: For 'more options' it appends three additional candidates; for other hints it clears the list and streams three new candidates matching the hint.

symbols.does: Routes a whole-doc transform choice into the candidate gallery.
symbols.description: It closes the transform menu and either runs a direct engine target or creates a doc.transform target for runGallery, using the transform label as the gallery title.

symbols.does: Handles keyboard shortcuts for undo, redo, duplicate, navigation, and deselection.
symbols.description: This listener ignores input fields and contentEditable elements, then maps Cmd/Ctrl+Z, Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Z/Y, Cmd/Ctrl+D, arrow keys, and Escape to the corresponding editor actions.

symbols.does: Initiates a new doc draft for a selected type.
symbols.description: It stores the chosen type and resets the brief, then sets default kind/system values for widget and wizard drafts while leaving them null for deck and brochure.

symbols.does: Creates a new doc, optionally with AI-generated content, and opens it for editing.
symbols.description: It builds an id, calls Vi drafting helpers when a brief is provided, falls back to blank pages on failure, saves the new doc, and switches to the edit view with reset editor state.

symbols.does: Opens an existing doc in the edit view.
symbols.description: It sets the active doc id, switches to edit view, resets the current page and selection, and clears undo/redo stacks.

symbols.does: Returns from edit view to the doc list.
symbols.description: It resets view to list, clears the active doc id, and clears the selected section index.

symbols.does: Applies an updater to the current doc and saves it.
symbols.description: It accepts either a new doc value or an updater function, applies it to the current doc, and calls saveDoc with the result.

symbols.does: Restores the previous doc snapshot from the undo stack.
symbols.description: It pops the current snapshot onto the redo stack, restores the prior snapshot via saveDoc, and ignores the resulting change to avoid re-recording it.

symbols.does: Restores the next doc snapshot from the redo stack.
symbols.description: It pops the next snapshot onto the undo stack and restores it via saveDoc, ignoring the resulting change to avoid re-recording it.

symbols.does: Replaces a single section within a specific page.
symbols.description: It returns an updated doc where only the section at the given page and section index is replaced with newSec.

symbols.does: Removes a section from a specific page.
symbols.description: It filters out the section at the given index and clears the selected section index.

symbols.does: Inserts a new default section of a given kind at a page index.
symbols.description: It looks up the block defaults in window.WS_BLOCKS, creates a new section with a generated id, and splices it into the page's section list.

symbols.does: Reorders sections within a page.
symbols.description: It removes the section at fromIdx and inserts it at toIdx, then updates the selected index to the new position.

symbols.does: Clones a section and inserts it immediately after the original.
symbols.description: It copies the section data, assigns a new id, and splices the duplicate into the section list.

symbols.does: Toggles the locked flag on a section.
symbols.description: It flips the locked boolean for the target section, which is used to block Vi and manual edits.

symbols.does: Inserts a new page into the doc, optionally from a layout.
symbols.description: It creates a blank page or builds one from window.WS_buildPage, splices it at the requested index, and sets the current page to that index.

symbols.does: Clones a page and inserts it after the original.
symbols.description: It copies the page, regenerates ids for the page and all its sections, splices the duplicate, and advances the current page.

symbols.does: Removes a page after confirmation, preventing removal of the last page.
symbols.description: It checks that more than one page exists, asks for confirmation, filters the page out, and adjusts the current page index.

symbols.does: Reorders pages in the doc.
symbols.description: It removes the page at fromIdx and inserts it at toIdx, then sets the current page to the new index.

symbols.does: Sets the kind of a page and clears its motif.
symbols.description: It updates the page's kind field while resetting motif to null, allowing the motif to be re-derived or reselected.

symbols.does: Sets the motif for a specific page.
symbols.description: It updates the page's motif field, which drives background and bleed styling through window.WS_MOTIFS.

symbols.does: Sets the document-wide theme and shows a toast.
symbols.description: It updates the doc's theme field and displays a toast with the theme name from window.WS_THEMES.

symbols.does: Inserts a section object at a specific index, merging provided data with defaults.
symbols.description: It resolves the block defaults for the section kind, creates a new section with merged data, and splices it into the page at the requested position.

symbols.does: Replaces the current page's kind and sections with a built layout.
symbols.description: It builds a page from window.WS_buildPage and overwrites the current page in the doc, then toasts the applied layout name.

symbols.does: Resolves a library payload and applies it as a layout or section insertion.
symbols.description: It uses window.WS_resolveLibraryPayload to interpret the payload; layouts replace the current page, while sections are appended to the current page.

symbols.does: Handles dropping a library payload onto the slide preview area.
symbols.description: It reads the custom drag data type, parses the payload, delegates to handleLibraryInsert, and clears the drop-active state.

symbols.does: Asks Claude to revise a single section's data based on a user message.
symbols.description: It builds a prompt with block type, current JSON data, and user request, calls window.claude.complete, parses the response, and merges the revised data into the section.

symbols.does: Asks Claude to generate three variations of a section.
symbols.description: It prompts for alternative versions of the block, parses the returned variations array, and stores them keyed by section id for the variations UI.

symbols.does: Applies or clears a selected variation for a section.
symbols.description: It updates the section data with the chosen variation and records the active variation index, or resets to the original when null is chosen.

symbols.does: Asks Claude to compose blocks for an empty slide.
symbols.description: It builds a prompt from document title and prior slide context, requests 2-4 sections, validates kinds against window.WS_BLOCKS, and replaces the slide's sections.

symbols.does: Renders built-in doc type cards plus registry-authored custom types.
symbols.description: It maps DOC_TYPES to clickable cards with icons or TypeThumb previews, then renders a separate group for extra registry types with provenance badges and family fallbacks.

symbols.does: Renders the new-doc drafting panel with brief input and kind/system pickers.
symbols.description: It shows a textarea for the brief, conditionally renders DraftPicker controls for widget kind/system or wizard kind, and provides AI draft and blank-start buttons.

symbols.does: Renders a scaled 16:9 slide frame with motif layer and brand chrome.
symbols.description: It uses a ResizeObserver to fit the slide to its container, applies a motif background layer, renders children, and adds page numbers plus a hatched bottom edge and brand mark.

symbols.does: Calculates and sets the slide frame scale based on container width.
symbols.description: This callback inside SlideFrame's ResizeObserver computes scale as container width divided by 1280, clamped between 0.2 and 1.4.

symbols.does: Wraps brochure page content in a simple frame.
symbols.description: A minimal component that renders children inside a styled container for one-page brochure previews.

symbols.does: Renders available layouts and AI-driven fill/propose actions for empty pages.
symbols.description: It lists layouts from window.WS_layoutsForType or WS_LAYOUTS, lets the user pick one, and offers buttons for Vi layout proposals and Vi auto-fill.

symbols.does: Renders the sections of a page with add-dividers between them.
symbols.description: It maps page.sections to SectionDraggable components and inserts window.WSAddDivider elements before, between, and after sections for adding new blocks.

symbols.does: Renders an individual draggable section with toolbar controls and variation chips.
symbols.description: It displays the block renderer, a drag handle, move/lock/duplicate/remove buttons, refine and vary actions, and a variation chip bar when the section is selected.

symbols.does: Renders a horizontal thumbnail strip of deck pages.
symbols.description: It shows a thumbnail for each page with motif preview, drag reordering, duplicate/remove actions, and an add button.

symbols.does: Renders a vertical outline of deck pages.
symbols.description: It lists pages with their first text headline, kind label, drag reordering, click-to-navigate, and an add-slide button.

symbols.does: Provides a workspace variables panel with add, edit, audit, and reference counts.
symbols.description: It displays variables from window.useWSVars, lets users add or edit values, shows live reference counts, and runs a Vi audit that proposes new variables to extract.

symbols.does: Recomputes variable reference counts from the global WS_VARS service.
symbols.description: This callback updates the local refs state by calling window.WS_VARS.countReferences and is invoked on mount and on doc/variable subscriptions.

symbols.does: Adds a new workspace variable after normalizing its key.
symbols.description: It lowercases and sanitizes the key, builds a default label, stores the variable via window.WS_VARS.setOne, and clears the add form.

symbols.does: Triggers a Vi audit that scans docs and proposes variables.
symbols.description: It calls window.WS_VARS.audit, stores the result, and shows a toast if the audit fails.

symbols.does: Adds a single audited variable to the workspace store.
symbols.description: It normalizes the variable key, stores it with window.WS_VARS.setOne, and removes it from the pending audit result list.

symbols.does: Accepts every variable proposed by the audit.
symbols.description: It iterates over auditResult.variables and calls acceptAudit for each, then clears the audit result.

symbols.does: Fetches and displays heuristic and Vi-generated suggestions for the current context.
symbols.description: It computes instant heuristic suggestions, then asynchronously requests Vi suggestions, filters dismissed ones, and renders clickable suggestion buttons that invoke runGallery.

symbols.does: Removes a suggestion from the rail and records it as dismissed.
symbols.description: It calls window.WS_AI.dismissSuggestion and filters the suggestion out of local state so it no longer appears.

symbols.does: Modal for reviewing extracted template variables and preview before saving.
symbols.description: It shows the template name, editable variable keys/defaults/kinds, a live materialized preview, and confirm/cancel actions while extraction is in progress.

symbols.does: Renders a materialized template preview for deck, brochure, widget, or wizard docs.
symbols.description: It materializes the template with current defaults and delegates to WSRenderedPagePreview, WidgetRender, or a wizard step list depending on the doc kind.

symbols.does: Renders a button-group picker for widget/wizard kind and system options.
symbols.description: It displays a label and a row of selectable option buttons, each showing a label and short description, with 'auto' as the default selection.

symbols.does: Bar for selecting document theme, page kind, and page motif.
symbols.description: It renders theme chips with color swatches, a page-kind dropdown, and a motif dropdown, plus a close button.

symbols.does: Button that opens a popover menu of available block kinds.
symbols.description: It toggles a small popover listing window.WS_BLOCKS entries and calls onAdd with the chosen block kind.

symbols.does: Asks Claude to generate a full set of pages/sections from a user brief.
symbols.description: It builds a detailed prompt with available block kinds, data shapes, brand voice, and slide/page rhythm, then parses the returned JSON into page and section objects with generated ids.

symbols.does: Returns default empty page structures for a doc type.
symbols.description: It produces a single page with appropriate kind and optional default hero section for brochures, or an empty page for decks, widgets, and wizards.

symbols.does: Returns default extra fields for widget or wizard docs.
symbols.description: It calls window.blankWidget or window.blankWizard with default kind/system values when no specific kind is selected.

symbols.does: Returns a default title string for a given doc type.
symbols.description: A small mapping that produces 'Untitled widget/wizard/deck/brochure' based on the type argument.

symbols.does: Truncates a brief to at most six words for use as a title.
symbols.description: It splits the brief on whitespace, takes the first six words, and appends an ellipsis if the original was longer.

symbols.does: Attempts to parse a string as JSON, falling back to extracting the first {...} block.
symbols.description: It first tries JSON.parse on the whole reply; if that fails it matches the first object literal and parses that, returning null if both attempts fail.
